pedagogyFrameworks.label: Cognitive Load Theory
pedagogyFrameworks.author: Sweller
pedagogyFrameworks.description: Minimise the mental effort required to process new material. When extraneous load is reduced — through clearer worked examples, better chunking, and fewer distractions — students can direct more attention to understanding. AI can handle low-value cognitive tasks so teachers and students focus on what matters.

pedagogyFrameworks.label: Depth of Knowledge
pedagogyFrameworks.author: Webb
pedagogyFrameworks.description: A framework that moves students from surface recall (DOK 1) through skill application (DOK 2) and strategic thinking (DOK 3) to extended reasoning and real-world transfer (DOK 4). AI tools can generate tasks at specific DOK levels, helping teachers design intentional progressions beyond surface recall.

pedagogyFrameworks.label: Universal Design for Learning
pedagogyFrameworks.author: CAST
pedagogyFrameworks.description: Design lessons from the outset with multiple means of engagement, representation, and expression. AI can rapidly produce alternative formats and differentiated materials, making the UDL principle of proactive flexibility practical at classroom scale without adding hours of preparation.

pedagogyFrameworks.label: Differentiated Instruction
pedagogyFrameworks.author: Tomlinson
pedagogyFrameworks.description: Match content, process, and product to each learner's readiness, interest, and learning profile. AI can instantly produce levelled texts, tiered tasks, and scaffolded resources — reducing the time cost that has historically made sustained differentiation difficult to maintain.

pedagogyFrameworks.label: Visible Learning
pedagogyFrameworks.author: Hattie
pedagogyFrameworks.description: Hattie's synthesis of 1,400+ meta-analyses identifies the instructional moves that most reliably accelerate learning: specific feedback, deliberate practice, metacognitive reflection, goal-setting. AI can speed up the feedback cycle and help students make their own thinking visible.

pedagogyFrameworks.label: Bloom's Taxonomy
pedagogyFrameworks.author: Bloom
pedagogyFrameworks.description: A hierarchy of cognitive skills — Remembering, Understanding, Applying, Analysing, Evaluating, Creating — that helps teachers design tasks with intentional depth. AI can generate questions and activities mapped to specific Bloom's levels, pushing students beyond surface recall toward higher-order thinking.

useCategories.label: Lesson Planning
useCategories.description: Draft lesson outlines, generate learning intentions, build activity sequences, and construct unit plans faster — then refine the AI output to your class context and curriculum requirements.

useCategories.label: Assessment & Feedback
useCategories.description: Generate rubric drafts, create quiz and test questions, produce feedback comment starters, and speed up the marking and reporting cycle without reducing the quality of the response.

useCategories.label: Differentiation
useCategories.description: Adapt the same curriculum content for different readiness levels, produce tiered tasks, and create accessible versions of texts and activities in the time it used to take to do one version.

useCategories.label: Student Research
useCategories.description: Support students to interrogate sources, synthesise information, ask better questions, and build research skills — with AI as a thinking partner rather than a shortcut to answers.

useCategories.label: Creative Projects
useCategories.description: Generate visual assets, draft story starters, produce design options, explore creative variations, and support creative work across Arts, English, and cross-curricular projects.

useCategories.label: Reading & Literacy
useCategories.description: Generate levelled reading texts, create comprehension questions, produce vocabulary scaffolds, and build literacy materials matched to where students actually are.

useCategories.label: Writing Support
useCategories.description: Produce writing frames and model texts, give structural feedback on drafts, scaffold the planning-to-draft process, and help students produce more polished work with less intervention per student.

useCategories.label: Class Engagement
useCategories.description: Create interactive activities, discussion prompts, warm-up tasks, and formative checks that make thinking visible and keep students actively participating in learning.

useCategories.label: Admin & Communication
useCategories.description: Draft newsletters, parent communications, report comment starters, meeting agendas, and internal documentation more consistently and in less time.

useCategories.label: Professional Learning
useCategories.description: Engage with research and professional reading more efficiently, prepare for appraisal conversations, explore pedagogical ideas, and reflect on your teaching practice.

domains.label: Curriculum & Planning
domains.description: Designing and planning learning — lesson plans, units and differentiated materials.

domains.label: Classroom Teaching
domains.description: Live teaching and learning — engagement, literacy, writing and student research.

domains.label: Assessment
domains.description: Checking and growing learning — assessment tasks, feedback and reporting.

domains.label: Creativity & Media
domains.description: Producing visual, audio and multimedia assets for teaching and student work.

domains.label: Administration
domains.description: The operational load around teaching — communications and documentation.

domains.label: Professional Learning
domains.description: Your own growth — research, reflection and preparing for appraisal.

cewaStatuses.label: Approved
cewaStatuses.description: Cleared for use on CEWA devices

cewaStatuses.label: Under Review
cewaStatuses.description: CEWA assessment in progress — avoid for now

cewaStatuses.label: Unreviewed
cewaStatuses.description: Not on CEWA list — personal devices only

accessTiers.label: Free tier + paid
accessTiers.description: Usable for free; a paid plan adds capacity or features.

accessTiers.label: Free for educators
accessTiers.description: Free with a verified school or teacher account.

accessTiers.label: School licence
accessTiers.description: Available when your school or system subscribes.

tools.name: ChatGPT
tools.description: Draft lesson resources, adapt explanations for your class, generate discussion questions, and create feedback starting points. One of the most versatile AI tools across every teaching role.
tools.notes: Most capable for open-ended tasks. Requires careful prompting for pedagogically sound outputs.

tools.name: Gemini
tools.description: Plan lessons, draft communications, and generate curriculum materials directly within Google Docs and Slides. A practical AI starting point for teachers already working in Google Workspace.
tools.notes: Deep integration with Google Docs, Slides, and Sheets makes it practical for curriculum planning.

tools.name: NotebookLM
tools.description: AI research and note-taking tool that works directly with your documents. Upload curriculum materials and interrogate them.
tools.notes: Particularly strong for synthesis and analysis tasks. Grounds responses in uploaded sources — reduces hallucination risk.

tools.name: Canva AI
tools.description: AI-powered design tools within Canva — generate images, presentations, and visual resources for the classroom.
tools.notes: CEWA-approved. Strong for visual learners and accessible resource creation. Image generation requires content moderation awareness.

tools.accessNote: Placeholder pending CEWA confirmation: available within the Microsoft 365 tenant most CEWA schools already use — no separate signup.
tools.name: Microsoft Copilot
tools.description: Microsoft's AI assistant, embedded across the 365 suite. Supports document drafting, summarisation, and data analysis in familiar tools.
tools.notes: CEWA-approved via Microsoft 365 licensing. Best starting point for teachers on CEWA devices.

tools.name: Khanmigo
tools.description: AI tutor and teaching assistant built specifically for education. Guides students through problems rather than giving answers.
tools.notes: Designed to scaffold rather than shortcut — strong alignment with intentional AI use philosophy. Socratic prompting built in.

tools.name: Diffit
tools.description: Generates differentiated reading materials at adjustable complexity levels from any topic, text, or URL.
tools.notes: Highly practical for mixed-ability classrooms. Outputs reading level-adjusted texts with comprehension questions instantly.

tools.name: Curipod
tools.description: AI-generated interactive lessons with polls, word clouds, and reflection prompts. Builds engagement and visible thinking.
tools.notes: Strong for formative checking and making student thinking visible. Lessons are editable after generation.

tools.name: Twee
tools.description: AI tool for English language teachers — generates questions, gap fills, multiple choice, and discussion prompts from any text.
tools.notes: Highly specific to English and EAL/D contexts. Saves significant time on comprehension and discussion task design.

tools.name: MagicSchool
tools.description: Purpose-built AI platform for educators with 60+ tools covering lesson planning, differentiation, report writing, and communication.
tools.notes: Education-specific guardrails and purpose-built workflows make it a strong starting point for teachers new to AI.

tools.name: Claude
tools.description: Draft detailed feedback, analyse curriculum documents, develop policy language, and think through complex professional scenarios. Strongest when tasks require careful, extended reasoning.
tools.notes: Particularly strong for tasks requiring careful reasoning — policy drafting, complex feedback, curriculum analysis. Less image capability than competitors.

tools.name: Suno
tools.description: Generate original songs, backing tracks, and audio stimulus from a text description. Create music for classroom listening, student creative projects, or arts unit resources.
tools.notes: Strong for creative arts and engagement. Content moderation required — outputs should be reviewed before classroom use.

tools.name: Adobe Firefly
tools.description: Create classroom-ready images, visual stimulus, and designed resources using Adobe's content-safe AI. Works inside familiar Creative Cloud tools teachers and students already use.
tools.notes: Children under 13 may use under teacher supervision with Creative Cloud. Never upload student photos or photos with personal/sensitive information.

tools.name: Chalkie AI
tools.description: Australian Curriculum-aligned lesson materials generated by AI, designed by teachers. Purpose-built for the Australian context.
tools.notes: Use Google SSO for authentication. Do not upload student personal or sensitive information. Must abide by CEWA's Generative AI Framework.

tools.accessNote: Placeholder pending CEWA confirmation: part of the Microsoft 365 / Reading Progress suite available to CEWA schools.
tools.name: Microsoft Reading Coach
tools.description: Personalised, AI-powered reading fluency practice for all learners. Adapts to each student's reading level and progress.
tools.notes: CEWA approved. Part of the Microsoft 365 Education suite.

tools.name: QuillBot
tools.description: AI-powered writing tool for paraphrasing, grammar checking, summarisation, and citation generation. Useful for teacher resource creation.
tools.notes: May only use Team Plan — schools may not use the free version.

tools.accessNote: Placeholder pending CEWA confirmation: bundled with SEQTA, which is used across many CEWA schools.
tools.name: SEQTA AI Assist
tools.description: AI-powered lesson planning and resource creation built into the SEQTA learning management system used across CEWA schools.
tools.notes: Check accuracy of AI-generated content. Use professional judgement on appropriateness for the intended lesson.

tools.name: Wayground (Quizizz)
tools.description: AI-supported game-based quizzes, personalised learning paths, and assessment tools. Formerly known as Quizizz.
tools.notes: Use CEWA SSO. Do not use personal or sensitive information — data stored offshore in the USA.

tools.name: Turnitin
tools.description: Plagiarism detection and AI writing detection platform. Includes Draft Coach for in-progress student writing feedback.
tools.notes: Be mindful of student information in submitted work. Includes Turnitin Draft Coach for citation and writing skills feedback.

tools.name: Speechify
tools.description: AI voice and text-to-speech tool. Converts any text to natural-sounding audio, supporting accessibility and differentiated learning.
tools.notes: May be used with caution. Strong accessibility tool for students who benefit from audio support.

tools.name: Nurture AI
tools.description: Plan lessons, design differentiated resources, and monitor student progress with an AI assistant built around evidence-based teaching frameworks. Keeps teachers in control while reducing preparation time.
tools.notes: CEWA pilot Jan–Jun 2025 for pilot schools only. Check with your school's digital team for current status.

tools.name: Perplexity AI
tools.description: AI-powered search and research assistant that cites its sources. Useful for teacher research and helping students understand how to evaluate information.
tools.notes: Not on CEWA list. Cites sources inline — strong model for teaching information literacy alongside AI use.

tools.name: Gamma
tools.description: AI presentation and document creation tool. Generate polished slides, documents, and webpages from a prompt or outline.
tools.notes: Not on CEWA list. Strong for teacher-created presentations and student projects.

tools.name: SchoolAI
tools.description: AI platform built specifically for K-12 classrooms. Teachers create custom AI spaces for students with guardrails and monitoring built in.
tools.notes: Not on CEWA list. Teacher-controlled student AI spaces with monitoring — strong alignment with intentional AI use philosophy.

tools.name: EduAide.ai
tools.description: AI teaching assistant with 100+ tools for lesson planning, assessment design, differentiation, and feedback generation.
tools.notes: Not on CEWA list. Education-specific with broad coverage of teaching tasks.

tools.name: DeepSeek
tools.description: General-purpose AI assistant not cleared for CEWA use. Avoid on school devices and networks. For similar capability, use Microsoft Copilot or ChatGPT with appropriate safeguards.
tools.notes: Not approved for CEWA use as per prime memo 7th February 2025.

tools.name: CapCut
tools.description: AI-powered video and photo editing app with automatic captions and text-to-speech features.
tools.notes: High risk — significant privacy, biometric and intellectual property concerns particularly regarding student use.
